title: DDL, Constraints, Schema Evolution, and Integrity Repair · Result-Grain Case: Referential Actions
summary: The support schema must reject impossible states and evolve while old application versions are still active. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql referential actions increment in a constraint-rich, reversible schema migration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose restrict, cascade, set-null, or set-default actions from lifecycle ownership semantics.
Enforce NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, primary-key, and foreign-key invariants at the database boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.

-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

steps.title: Build Referential Actions evidence · Theory Referential Actions
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-referential-actions-o5ceky" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; DDL constraint, migration, or integrity-repair evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-referential-actions-o5ceky
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
CREATE TABLE lab_assignments_o5ceky (
  ticket_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES tickets(ticket_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  assigned_at TEXT NOT NULL
);
